Brahmbhatt  The system is designed to be as minimal as possible, and have the tokens maintain a 1 token == $1 peg. This stablecoin has the following properties:  - Exogenous Collateral  - Dollar Pegged  - Algorithmically Stable  The Aurum Protocol should always be \"overcollateralized\". At no point, should the value of all the collateral (AUR) <= the dollar backed value of all the AUSD. params.amountCollateral: The amount of collateral to deposit

params.amountAUSDToMint: The amount of AUSD to mint
params.amountCollateral: The amount of collateral to deposit

returns._0: The address of the AurumUSD contract

params.user: The user to query for account information

returns._0: The amount of AUSD tokens a given user has minted

params.user: The user to query for account information

returns._0: The total collateral value in USD for a given user

params.user: The user to query for aggregated account information

returns._0: All account info (health factor, minted AUSD, and collateral value) for a user
The additional feed precision used to adjust the precision of Chainlink prices from 8 decimals to 18 decimals

params.user: The user to query for account information

returns._0: The total quantity of collateral tokens for a given user
The price feed address for the protocol

params.user: The user to query for account information

returns._0: The health factor value for a user
The percentage given to liquidators to incentivize clearing bad debt
A liquidation limit to prevent total user wipeouts on small dips
The precision divisor for liquidation percentage calculations
The percentage of collateral value counted as "safe" for backing debt
The maximum supply of AUSD allowed to be in circulation
A given debt amount where the close factor is ignored to allow full liquidation (cleaning dust)
The protocol's minimum required health factor
The standard precision used for AUSD calculations
The percentage of the liquidation bonus taken by the protocol as revenue
The amount of collateral tokens given a USD value
The USD value of a given amount of collateral tokens

params.debtToCover: The amount of AUSD to burn to improve the user's health factor
params.user: The user who has broken the health factor. Their health factor should be below MIN_HEALTH_FACTOR

params.amountAUSDToMint: The number of AUSD tokens to mint

params.amountCollateral: The amount of collateral to redeem

params.amountAUSDToBurn: The amount of AUSD to burn
params.amountCollateral: The amount of collateral to redeem

burnAUSD(uint256).notice: This burns a given amount of AUSD

depositCollateral(uint256).notice: This function will deposit a user's collateral into the protocol

depositCollateralAndMintAUSD(uint256,uin.notice: This function will deposit your collateral and mint the AUSD tokens in one transaction

liquidate(address,uint256).notice: You can partially liquidate another userYou will get a liquidation bonus for taking the user's fundsThis function assumes the protocol will be roughly 125% overcollateralized in order for this to work.A known bug would be if the protocol were 100% or less collateralized, then we wouldn't be able to incentivize the liquidators.         For example, if there was a "black swan" event causing a sharp drop in the price of gold, before anyone could be liquidated.

mintAUSD(uint256).notice: To mint AUSD, the user must have enough collateral to cover the minimum collateralization ratio of 125%

redeemCollateral(uint256).notice: To redeem collateral, the health factor must be at least 1 AFTER collateral is pulled

redeemCollateralAndBurnAUSD(uint256,uint.notice: This function burns AUSD and redeems underlying collateral in one transaction
